entry.title: Re: Aggregation of rate elements with rate subelements Su...
entry.content:
Hi Elena,

I'm happy to discuss this here in the community.
On the first glance the design of the rate plan template with two rate elements with each having 2 rate subelements does not seem to be optimal.
However, I do not fully understand the required outcome.

If I understand you right, your external system will send 4 kind of usage records:
1. units with active drivers
2. units with inactive drivers
3. units with active vehicles
4, units with inactive vehicles

And you want to have exactly corresponding 4 different charges on the bill. At least that would be the result if you use the charge definitions you described.

Is this the expected structure of the bill?
Or would you expect a bill with one charge for vehicle&drivers and another one for active&inactive?

Kind regards,
Carsten

PS:
When Subscription Billing is rating usage, the price calculation happens always on the level of a charge. E.g., if you send a usage record with active drivers, then only this specific charge is calculated.
With that, the aggregation rule cannot be used to dynamically aggregate quantities across charges.
 

PS2:
Regarding the problem with a fixed rate for tier 1 to 19, you could adjust the suitable quantity before applying the price condition in the Calculate Price Element function.
A typical strategy could be:
a) have a lookup table with the minimum quantity (e.g. 19 EA) as an output column. Replace EA if you use another one for the rate element.
b) determine highest value of the quantity and the minimum quantity using the Suitable Quantity operator.
c) use this suitable quantity to calculate the price element. The price condition type Single with a linear price type would do the job.

entry.title: S/4HANA - Embedded Analytics: Extensibility Process & Custom CDS View
entry.content:
Overview :

In this blog, we will explore the Extensibility Process and focus on developing custom CDS views using S/4HANA Embedded Analytics. The Extensibility process flow is the key approach used by analytical specialists to develop new Analytical reports and KPIs in the S/4HANA system. SAP presents many standard VDMs to meet the requirements of varying customers and industries. However, there are some specific use cases that demand customized CDS views for their business requirements.

Extensibility Process Flow Diagram:

As depicted in the above diagram, once we identify the standard VDM views from the view browser app, we can create a new custom VDM views to meet the specific requirement of the business. The new custom CDS view, will be used as a source for creating analytical queries. The analytical specialist can create Reports, KPIs and multidimensional reports using these analytical queries.

Step 1:

In S/4HANA Homepage, navigate to “Custom CDS Views App” and proceed  to create a new custom CDS view.

Precondition to create Custom CDS view:

  • Using SAP Released standard CDS views.
  • Using Previously published custom CDS Views

In this CDS view, we have used two standard CDS views and join them using the common key fields. Primary CDS view: overdue payments CDS view and join it with the Secondary CDS view: customer dimension view. We will also create a calculated field to derive ‘high risk’ and ‘low risk’ based on the outstanding amount.

Step 2:

Select the scenario type as Analytical cube and provide the basic details of the Custom CDS view. Custom CDS view name will be prefixed with ‘YY1’*. There is other scenario type also we can select, as shown below.

The Next screen will be presented with 5 sections. We need to maintain all these sections, namely “Data sources”, “Parameters”, “Elements”, “Element Properties”, “Filters”.

As shown above , In the  “Data sources” a section, we will click the “Add” button on the top right corner of the table. Select the primary data sources from the list of released views. Also select the Associate data sources and provide the join condition.

Step 3:

The Next section is “Parameters” section, In Most cases CDS views will produce empty records, if not maintained the parameter values correctly. For example, in the standard cds view (shown below), there are list of parameters maintained, that we need to assign values to fetch the desired results.

In this example we have provided the values for display currency and ExchangeRateType to provide the valid output.

Step 4:

In the “Elements” section, we will select the required attributes of the primary data source fields and associate data source fields. As pointed out below, there are elements, calculations, and parameter options.

Once you have selected the required fields from the “Elements” option, additional calculated fields can be created using the “Calculation” option.

Step 5:

As mentioned in the above step, select the calculation option, and provide a meaning full name for the calculated field. In this case we are trying to find out Credit Risk by considering the outstanding due amount. Hence “Z_CD_CreditRisk” variable uses the “CASE” function. We applied the logic - if overdue amount more than 10000 it will be termed as “High credit risk”.  

Please note the Functions options provides lot of flexibility in applying logics. We can use these calculated fields in our custom cds view reports. The output of this calculated fields is clearly shown in this blog's output section.

Step 6:

In the filter section, we can restrict the output by any selected field available from the report. In this example we have we have restricted overdue amount greater than 3000 euros to be shown in the report.

Step 7:

After Providing required information in these 5 sections, procced to “Check” the report for any issues or syntax errors. This option is available in the bottom of the table section. After the confirmation message, we can procced to publish the report. This will notify us “Publishing successful” as shown below.

The Output of the newly created custom cds view will look like the one below.  You can notice the calculated field “Z_CD_CreditRisk” is featuring in the report.  Based on the overdue amount it updates the risk level in the report.

Conclusion:

This blog provides us with clarity on how to build and use “custom cds views” in a step-by-step manner. This demonstrates the S/4HANA Embedded Analytics capability in a simple-to-use and easy-to-implement way for our SAP customers. Hope our customers will greatly benefit from this embedded analytics capability of SAP S/4HANA for their dynamic business requirements.

Thank You for your time!!!

entry.title: Re: Adapt UI depending on user role
entry.content:
Hallo Katharina,

unfortunately this is not possible.
The UI adaptions you do as an admin are reflected for all users.
The authorizations you can configure via business roles are not influencing the UI behaviour. If you provide for example the marketing area for Germany, then you would see in the Contacts app only the interaction contacts for Germany (data access), but you can't control fields/tabs/screens with it.

Best regards,
Jan

entry.title: Re: Custom data as table, CDS, Domain, Business object and all that jazz...
entry.content:
Great blog @mlauber , it really helps putting things into perspective. Thanks!

Not sure whether the system has changed in the meantime, but the example above of integrating the Custom Business Object CDS with other custom CDS views does not seem to work (anymore). Which is unfortunate, since that's the one thing I would like to do here.

Perhaps I'm just missing a small step, but the end result is that it does not work.

On our customers' S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, I have created a Custom Business Object, YY1_ABONNEMENTPRODUCT. It links a reusable custom code list with a product and has been populated with four lines.

User Interface has been checked, giving me the nice SM30-like app. Backend service has been checked as well. "Can Be Associated" seems only to be relevant for other business objects, but for sake of testing is checked as well. The object has been published.

I can preview the data in the CDS from Eclipse just fine. However whenever I try to join or associate with that CDS from my custom CDS, it is not permitted.

Most information I can find on the issue, refers me to the difference between Key User Extensibility (the Custom Business Object) on the customizing client and Developer Extensibility (the Custom CDS views) on the development client.

I've been creating all kinds of custom business scenarios, communication arrangements, consumption models, OData services and so far it seems like a lot of work for a simple data integration.

Please tell me this is not another limitation of the public cloud environment and I've just missed something simple 🙂 Thanks in advance for your help and insights!

entry.title: Re: Aggregation of rate elements with rate subelements Su...
entry.content:
Dear Carsten,

thank you very much for your answer, this is already very helpful.

The goals I'm trying to achieve are the following:

1. Avoid overcomplicating the set up and future maintenance of rate elements.
2. Set up rate elements in a way to manage usage records sent from the external system in a uniform way for all the rate plans.
3. Create an invoice with the lowest number of lines possible.

Unfortunately, I think it will be difficult to achieve all the 3 goals together but if you have any idea I'm all ears 🙂 

Yes, the external system will send 4 kind of usage records as you described, and the reason why I thought we need to set up 2 rate elements with 2 subelements each is because we have other rating strategies like the following:

- Different price for driver and for vehicle. Same price for active or inactive units (no aggregation, unit price is applied to total qty).
- Different price for driver and for vehicle. Different price for inactive units (no aggregation, unit price is applied to total qty).
- Fixed price for active drivers & vehicles. Different price for inactive units (no aggregation, unit price is applied to total qty).

I thought that using two rate elements (driver & vehicle) with 2 subelements (active & inactive) was a better solution than creating 4 rate elements (ex. driver active, driver inactive, etc.), if you think otherwise I can go back on this decision (I'm still working in a DEV system).

Concerning the bill structure, ideally when the same price is applied to all the rate elements and subelements, only one line is necessary. On the other hand, when different prices are applied, multiple line should be printed.
I already did some research and I'm afraid that having one line per rate element/subelement is the only solution that will fit all the scenarios.

Thank you for clarifying why the aggregation is not working right know, I think I understand the logic better now but unfortunately I still don't see how I can achieve the scenario "For the usage of 0 to 19 units (both "drivers" and "vehicle", both "active" and "inactive") a fixed amount is billed every month, ex. 50€ + From the 20th units on, a price per unit is billed in addition to the fixed price, ex. 5€/1ea. The same price is billed for "drivers" and "vehicle", "active" and "inactive"."
Based on the info provided on the other pricing scenario we have to achieve in parallel, do you maybe have an idea on how to achieve this?

Thank you also for the tip on the fixed rate for tier 1 to 19, I'll test it 🙂

Thank you very much in advance for your help.

Kr,
Elena

entry.title: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 3 - Integration via REST API
entry.content:
Another option for integrating SAP AI Core service into your SAP Build Apps application is to use the existing core flow function "Create Record" via BTP destination REST API integration.

1. Configure the BTP destination REST API Integration in SAP Build Apps. You need to configure the additional common request headers and resource schema.

 

2. Design your page and UI controls in SAP Build Apps, then open the logic canvas to add logic to the desired UI control, include the "Create Record" flow function in the logic, and configure the input and output parameters accordingly.

 

3. Use the Create Record flow function to consume the new REST API Integration. Please note that although we use the "Create Record flow function," we are not actually creating any new records in the backend AI service. Instead, we use this flow function to send a "Post" request, allowing the AI service to receive the "prompt" and respond accordingly. After receiving the response from the AI service, we need to further process the response, e.g., parse it and assign variables.

 

 

4. Deploy your app and run it

Link to previous blog: SAP Build Apps integration with SAP AI Core services: Part 2 - Integration via Javascript function

entry.title: Grouping by Codelist Value in Fiori List report - Unnecessary duplication of title
entry.content:
Hi, 

in our applications we decided that for code-lists we do not want to display the code on the UI. 

Now all my codes are annotated like this: 

planningStatus                @Common.Text           : planningStatus.descr
                              @Common.TextArrangement: #TextOnly;

In my app I am choosing to group against this planning status. But since I only have the description on my UI I can just group by this element then as specified in my UI service:

// Default grouping and sorting
PresentationVariant     : {
  GroupBy       : [planningStatus.descr],
  SortOrder     : [{
    Property  : displayId,
    Descending: true
  }],
  Visualizations: ['@UI.LineItem']
},

Now in the UI the title of the field is built like this: 

 
 

It seems, that for the title, the "Common.Text*" Annotations are ignored. To remove the title in brackets, I must display the code and group against it. That means I cannot go with #TextOnly. 

Is there a way to solve this without using custom code in my fiori application to format/set the title of the control (https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/api/sap.m.GroupHeaderListItem)?

entry.title: Empowering Public Sector Users of SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Business AI
entry.content:
For many governments around the world, driving efficiency and leveraging technological advances to increase workforce productivity are high on their list of priorities. At SAP, we harness the right AI tools working within the context of your data, to offer you real-world results that make a difference to your business. This is what we refer to as SAP Business AI and SAP Joule is your business AI assistant, grounded in SAP data. 

After the 2408 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, government agencies can look forward to AI innovations (SAP Road Map Explorer) that SAP is planning to introduce next in Public Sector Management (PSM). The PSM scope bundle consists of Grants Management, Budget Management and Funds Accounting. Specifically, SAP will be adding budget and grant management context to SAP Joule. With context awareness, SAP Joule is designed to streamline and improve the user experience, allowing users to efficiently navigate their financial systems and retrieve information to make better strategic decisions. New users typically require extensive training, SAP Joule can quickly provide definitions, summaries, and how-to guidance, saving time and reducing enquiries to IT departments.

Let us have a look at what these planned changes entail.

The User Experience - Reimagined

Interactions with SAP Joule are carried out using natural language, just like how you communicate with your friendly colleagues each day! No learning curve to overcome before you get the answers you need. 

Use SAP Joule to get quick answers to your questions and save time searching through lengthy . No question is too basic for Joule and Joule can even make sense of your shorthand!
  

Efficient Navigation and Task Execution

Today, SAP supports many customers across the globe who run highly complex business processes. Users often utilize different functions and screens to perform multiple transactions. SAP Joule can help users quickly and efficiently navigate to relevant applications to complete their tasks. In our Early Adopter program, participants have reported time savings of up to 90% when performing navigational and transactional tasks. With Joule’s help, a task that used to require 5 minutes was reduced to 30 seconds. This can potentially lead to significant cost savings for public sector organizations. Let’s estimate the potential savings an agency with 10,000 employees could realize: 

 

Joule can help reduce the time to perform routine tasks by quickly bringing you to the relevant application screen. Instead of navigating through menu options, simply enter a short query such as “Show Funds”. You will be provided with options to access the relevant screen directly or to read related documentation. 

Click on the “Open” button and SAP Joule directly brings you to the “Manage Funds” screen where you can search and display information about various funds. You can create or delete funds, or access individual funds to make changes.

A new user can quickly learn about the “Manage Funds” application by clicking on the SAP Help link provided by Joule.

Here’s another example of how you can easily navigate the application and perform your tasks more quickly with the help of SAP Joule:

To make changes to a grant, simply say what you want to do and SAP Joule will guide you.

When you click on the “Open” button to manage grants, SAP Joule brings you directly to the screen where you can view or amend details of the grant.

A user who is unfamiliar with Grants Management can learn more by clicking on the SAP Help link provided by . Joule ensures that the most relevant content is presented to you right from the start, saving you time scrolling through multiple pages of less important information.


We are just starting our journey with SAP Joule, and our customers can expect more AI innovations to be added across SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Information on these plans can be found on the SAP Road Map Explorer. To find out more about AI and its potential to serve your needs in the public sector, please read our whitepaper, “AI in the Public Sector”.

                <Property Name="MessageV4" Type="Edm.String" Nullable="false" MaxLength="50" sap:label="Message Variable" sap:creatable="false" sap:updatable="false" sap:sortable="false" sap:filterable="false"/>

            </ComplexType>

I try to create the List Page table based on PIDLIST_NEW entity, but should also expose fields from the Associations in PLISTOVERSN, which is an association itself in PIDLIST_NEW.

I have following annotations.cds file so far:

annotate PidService.PID with @(
    UI                            : {
        LineItem       : [
            {
                $Type                : 'UI.DataField',
                Value                : PLISTOVERSN.Clmno,
                ![@HTML5.CssDefaults]: {width: 'auto'}
            },
            {
                $Type                : 'UI.DataField',
                Value                : Refno,
                ![@HTML5.CssDefaults]: {width: 'auto'}
            },
            {
                $Type                : 'UI.DataField',
                Value                : HPntext,
                ![@HTML5.CssDefaults]: {width: 'auto'}
            },
            {
                $Type                : 'UI.DataField',
                Value                : Clmty,
                ![@HTML5.CssDefaults]: {width: 'auto'}
            },
              {
                $Type                : 'UI.DataField',
                Value                :  PLISTOVERSN.NVERSNTOSORG.Refdt,
                ![@HTML5.CssDefaults]: {width: 'auto'}
            },
            {
                $Type                : 'UI.DataField',
                Value                : PLISTOVERSN.NVERSNTOSORG.Cbactiondt,
                ![@HTML5.CssDefaults]: {width: 'auto'}
            }
        ],
        SelectionFields: [
            Clmno,
            HPntext,
            Refno,
            Clmty,
            PLISTOVERSN.NVERSNTOSORG.Refdt,
            PLISTOVERSN.NVERSNTOSORG.Cbactiondt,
        ],
        HeaderInfo     : {
            $Type         : 'UI.HeaderInfoType',
            TypeName      : '{i18n>pidlist.title}',
            TypeNamePlural: '{i18n>pidlist.title}',
            Title         : {Value: Clmno},
            Description   : {Value: HPntext},
            TypeImageUrl : '{icon}',
        },
    },
    Capabilities                  : {
        InsertRestrictions.Insertable: true,
        UpdateRestrictions.Updatable : true,
        DeleteRestrictions.Deletable : false
    },
    UI.DataPoint #HeaderFacetVersn: {
        $Type: 'UI.DataPointType',
        Title: PLISTOVERSN.Clmno,
        Value: PLISTOVERSN.Versn
    },
    UI.HeaderFacets               : [
        {
            $Type : 'UI.ReferenceFacet',
            Target: '@UI.DataPoint#HeaderFacetVersn',
        }
    ],
)

Which is making a correct request to the external service and I got response as:


{"@odata.context":"$metadata#PID(Clmno,Clmty,Refno,HPntext,PLISTOVERSN(Clmno,NVERSNTOSORG(Clmno,Refdt,Cbactiondt)))","@odata.metadataEtag":"W/\"05c889be296c72581a2623a8fa0a5a312e1b3a97711d93906e4d96fde99f9387\"","@odata.count":"1","value":[{"Clmno":"200500152350","Clmty":"XRC2","Refno":"TES","HPntext":"TEST HEAD PK","PLISTOVERSN":[{"Clmno":"200500152350","NVERSNTOSORG":[{"Clmno":"200500152350","Refdt":"20240224","Cbactiondt":"20240316"},{"Clmno":"200500152350","Refdt":"20240224","Cbactiondt":"20240316"}]},{"Clmno":"200500152350","NVERSNTOSORG":[]}]}]}

So first version has 2 records in its association and the second 0. But when trying to display into the table it sets strange dots on the fields, and trying to make so kind of aggregation.

And I believe here I should have 3 lines:

1. First version with empty values for association fields.
2. Second version with first value for association fields.
3. Second version with second value for association fields.

Is this somehow achievable through annotations and Fiori Elements?

Thanks a lot and regards,
Violina

entry.title: Re: Excise and VAT solution for India in S4HANA Public cloud
entry.content:
Dear Varun,

Thank you for your question.

In Public Cloud, the best practice content for VAT is delivered with scope item J58 - Accounting and Financial Close. This is a standard scope item which is included in bundle Enterprise Management and can be activated for country India.

Please check if the best practice content delivered for country India cover all business requirements.

Thank you and best regards,

Nicoleta Cernea

entry.title: Re: Flow Builder Plus: the business insight, Part II
entry.content:
Hi @fadeevom 

Good point.

Simply, the FKONT determined by flow types. Flow type with 90* mapping to FKONT = 1, 80* mapping to FKONT = 2, 60* as FKONT = 3. Others are FKONT = 4. Empty means FKONT = 0.

We are now adding a separate page for Flow Type which contains the concept of Flow Type, Flow Level and FKONT and their linkages.

That official help documentation will available for public cloud release CE2502 and you can refer to it.

Thank you.

Best regards, Hongjun

entry.title: Enable Work Zone to Consume Business Solutions from Other BTP Subaccounts (content federation)
entry.content:
SAP Build Work Zone now allows for the creation of HTML5 Content Providers from any SAP BTP subaccount. In this session, you will explore how business apps can be developed such that they can be accessed by SAP Build Work Zone across subaccounts, enabling content federation for single-tenant and multi-tenant applications.

Speaker: Rupam Bhattacharya

Rupam has been a developer, technical consultant, and development architect, and currently is a senior product manager at SAP Build, focusing on helping developers make the most out of SAP Build Work Zone. He is very enthusiastic about AI, robotics and Lego.

entry.title: Leveraging Decisions and Business Rules in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
Unlock the potential of decisions in SAP Build Process Automation. In this joint session with SAP and Accenture, we delve into the evolution of business rules into Decisions and what SAP Build Process Automation now offers you. Explore various usage patterns for decisions, learn tips and tricks to create and deploy decisions, and discover how to integrate them into different applications, workflows, and processes. Wojciech from Accenture will a demo based on a real-world customer project. Finally, Sesh will discuss the future of Decisions, including generative AI and decision templates.

Speakers:

  • Wojciech Papuga, Accenture
  • Seshadri Ramanarayanan

Wojciech works at Accenture as an SAP developer, primarily on SAP cloud solutions designing workflows and applications (with SAPUI5) on the SAP BTP platform. He graduated with a degree in computer science from Lodz University of Technology, and in his free time is a computer gamer and F1 fan.

Seshadri is a senior product manager with SAP Build Process Automation, focusing on decisions / business rules, SAP S/4HANA extensibility, workflows/processes, and internal stakeholder management. With over 17 years of service at SAP, he brings a wealth of experience in business process management and business rule management to his role. Sesh has been a speaker at numerous conferences like Sapphire, TechEd, and bpmNext, sharing his knowledge in the area of process automation.

entry.title: SAP CAP: Error Starting Service After Deployment MODULE NOT FOUND requireStack
entry.content:
Hi experts,

We have a CAP services that is using a set of custom .js files.

We are getting the error below after deployment when trying to start the service.

"error":{"code":"MODULE_NOT_FOUND","requireStack":["/home/vcap/app/srv/utils/xmlUtils.js","/home/vcap/app/srv/utils/validationHeader.js","/home/vcap/app/srv/controller/validationHeader.js","/home/vcap/app/srv/edi-code-to-order-srv.js"]}

Odd thing is that if we execute the service from BAS everything works fine but not able to deploy.

We have an event handler implemented named edi-code-to-order-srv.js for our edi-code-to-order-srv.cds. Here we have an import of a custom .js.

const cds = require('@sap/cds')
const { executeHttpRequest } = require('@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client')
const { validationHeaderControl } = require('./controller/validationHeader');

This custom .js has other requires of other custom .js and it is place within the srv folder.

Our package.json

{
  "name": "fof-edi-quote-to-order-njs",
  "version": "1.0.0",
  "description": "xxxxx",
  "repository": "xxxxxxx",
  "license": "UNLICENSED",
  "private": true,
  "dependencies": {
    "@cap-js-community/odata-v2-adapter": "^1.13.1",
    "@sap-cloud-sdk/http-client": "^3.16.0",
    "@sap-cloud-sdk/resilience": "^3.16.0",
    "@sap/cds": "^8",
    "@sap/cds-hana": "^2",
    "@sap/hana-client": "^2.21.28",
    "@sap/xssec": "^3",
    "express": "^4",
    "hdb": "^0.19.9",
    "axios": "^1.7.2",
    "body-parser": "^1.20.2",
    "dotenv": "^16.4.5",
    "fast-xml-parser": "^4.4.0",
    "fs": "^0.0.1-security",
    "he": "^1.2.0",
    "jsonwebtoken": "^9.0.2",
    "moment": "^2.30.1"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@cap-js/sqlite": "^1",
    "@sap/cds-dk": "^7",
    "@sap/eslint-plugin-cds": "^3",
    "@sap/hdi-deploy": "4.9.5",
    "cds-swagger-ui-express": "^0.9.0",
    "eslint": "^9"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "start": "cds-serve"
  },
  "cds": {
    "requires": {
      "cpqApi": {
        "kind": "rest",
        "credentials": {
          "destination": "cpq-quote-api-i-http"
        }
      }
      "[production]": {
        "auth": "xsuaa"
      },
      "db": "hana"
    },
    "hana": {
      "deploy-format": "hdbtable"
    }
  }
}

Any idea what can be happenging?

Many thanks in advance!

C.

entry.title: Re: Consuming Microsoft 365 API in the Cloud Application Programming (CAP) Model
entry.content:
Today (2024/08), you can just use a CAP Destination, to call the ms graph REST api without additional dependencies, if your are using a technical user flow.

There is still the destination limitation for the MS OBO authentication flow (see SAP BTP Bridge).

Sadly, to be able to test your service call also locally, you have to use a hybrid CAP scenario, because the OAuth2ClientCredentials fetch token is part of the destination service (only cloud) and is not working locally!

But luckily, CAP easily supports hybrid dev by binding the nessessary service using 

cds bind -2 YOUR-DESTINATION-SERVICE
and starting it using the hybrid profile
cds watch --profile hybrid
 
CAP is getting better each month 😉 
 
Regards Holger

entry.title: Cross-Platform Mobile App Development Made Simple with SAP Build Code
entry.content:
Learn to create and enhance mobile apps for iOS and Android through a single code-line using SAP Build Code. This session covers getting started by creating your first app from the SAP Build Lobby and enhancing it in the Build Code development environment. Discover new debugging tools to monitor logs and network traces. The session will conclude by highlighting how you can build and ship your app to users using SAP Build Code.

Speaker: Bill Froelich

Bill Froelich is a product owner for the Mobile Development Kit within SAP Mobile Services. Building on his background managing, implementing, and deploying enterprise mobile solutions, he works closely with SAP teams, customers and partners to understand their needs and bring them into the product.

entry.title: Re: Caused by: EvalError: Refused to evaluate a string as...
entry.content:
This is most probably due to the preload bundle (Component-preload.js or library-preload.js) being generated into string which has to be eval'd at runtime. To avoid that, exclude the third party library from the bundle. See my other answer at Solved: HowTo use 3rd party lib with content-security-policy omitting eval()" - SAP Community.

If the issue still occurs even after excluding the third party lib from the bundle, follow my other Q&A How to Fix "String as JavaScript" Errors in FLP? (Async Module Loading) - Stack Overflow where I have summarized documented best practices for CSP compliance.

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite Monthly Webinar - August 2024
entry.content:
SAP Integration Suite is an integration platform-as-a-service (iPaaS) that enables the design, development, execution, governance, curation, analysis, and management of the full lifecycle of integration flows, APIs, and high-volume business events that connect on-premises and cloud-based processes, services, applications, and data across organizations. SAP Integration Suite is available on multiple cloud providers.

In this webinar, we'll have two sections:

Product Updates: Get updates on key capabilities and latest enhancements released for SAP Integration Suite.


OR

#AskAnythingIntegration: Our panel of experts will answer any questions you have on integration.

entry.title: Re: System table to view Remote Table Filters
entry.content:
Found my own solution, pasting here in case anyone needs it...

SELECT SCHEMA_NAME, VIEW_NAME, DEFINITION

FROM "SYS"."VIEWS"

WHERE 1=1

AND SCHEMA_NAME = 'EUK_SOURCE_TABLES'

AND DEFINITION LIKE '%MANDT%' AND DEFINITION NOT LIKE '%"MANDT" =%'

ORDER BY "VIEW_NAME";

entry.title: General Display Name Opt out in Second Half of 2024
entry.content:
General Display Name Opt out in Second Half of 2024

We are excited to announce the upcoming release for our opt out of General Display Name, so the employee name could be consistent globally in suite. Starting with the Second Half 2024 release, General Display Name will be enabled for all customers with the option to opt out. For the First Half 2025 release, General Display Name will become universal for all customers and the opt out option will be removed. 

 

General Display Name is release to customer two years ago. For now thousands of customers have enabled and been benefit from the feature. With General Display Name, customer can configure employee names that are expected to be displayed consistently across SAP SuccessFactors HXM Suite. A new admin option, Enable adoption of General Display Name, is added to control it. You can see this option under Admin Center -> Company System and Logo Settings. The new capability provides both consistency and flexibility to managing display names. With the new capability, general display names preferred by employees are displayed in all interactions where a legal name isn’t required, while administrators and HRBPs can also access a person’s legal name whenever needed. For details how to configure General Display Name please refer to https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/adopting-general-display-name/ba-p/13536203
 
If customer has already enabled General Display Name, nothing will be changed. If customer haven't enable General Display Name but configured name format on People Profile, the name format will be used for General Display Name. For non-EC customers and for EC customers who haven't configure any name format, the system calculates the value according to message key COMMON_USER_DISPLAYNAME in company default locale, by default the message key is defined as a combination of first name, middle name and last name. If there is no full name, middle name, and last name, the field value is set to Unknown. To migrate to General Display Name, we need to run a job to generate display name according to name format configuration. That will be scheduled after new release downtime upgrade. Details please refer to below migration timetable
 

Migration timetable

Based on current release schedule, we will plan to run the General Display Name migration job as below: JobType: Manage General Display Name

If the job is scheduled, you can see the scheduled job in Scheduled Job Manager. Track the status of the job in Scheduled Job Manager, you could have better idea when the upgrade is finished. 

Below is planned migration time for preview and production environment

Planned migration period for preview environment:

10/12/2024 12 : 00 AM to 10/19/2024 11:59 PM

Planned migration period for production environment:

11/16/2024 12 : 00 AM to 11/23/2024 11:59 PM

 

Have A Question Need Support?

If you encounter any issues while testing this feature, please create an incident with the component LOD-SF-PLT-MNG

 

Next Steps

• Once the Preview environment is upgraded with the 2H 2024 version, review the employee's name and consider if name format need to be configured, and prepare for the changes in Production
• Review the release note and the latest documentation in the Resources section below in order to understand the features supported
• Review and subscribe to the Customer Community blog to stay informed of the latest information on the change.
 

Resources

Customer Community Blog
https://community.sap.com/t5/human-capital-management-blogs-by-sap/adopting-general-display-name/ba-p/13536203
 
Help Portal
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PLATFORM/70097a1a469d47a0ae08809e4a240f98/ac31628b0f804c2689585b3aca96eed9.html

Migration Progress Update

Migration progress will be updated in below table

DateFinished DC

entry.title: Re: B1 Sap error -5002: 10000100 - Document total value m...
entry.content:
Hi @mihamuro ,

I'm not a technical expert, but your XML looks weird to me, in the row level bit specifically. From my understanding, ORDN should have RDN1 as its line-level child, not RPD1 as you shared here. Hence, DocTotal is 0 as there are no lines feeding it.

I hope it helps.

BR,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: Object Manager: Using PA20 or PA30. Hit List
entry.content:
Old question but the answer should be that you can set a parameter that removes the searchresult so you start with an empty hit list.
Save your searches as favorites to re run the as neede.

TC: SU3
Tab: Parameters 
Add parameter: OM_OBJM_NO_LAST_SEAR
Value: 1

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
How to create the best planning scenario to retain planning flexibility in S/4HANA and DM
entry.content:
Hello SAP DM Community,

 

Our digital manufacturing team is currently working on various planning scenarios to combine the advantages of SAP DM with the planning functions of S/4HANA in the best possible way.
We have realised that if we implement detailed planning in S/4HANA and keep the level of detail high, the advantages on the DM side are lost in detailed planning and the flexible switching back and forth between similar work centres. This is due to the fact that we cannot change the production version and therefore the routing for an order that we have already released and sent to DM.

 

It is therefore no longer possible to switch to another similar work centre in REO at short notice, as the master data only knows one work centre that we have specified in the initial production version and we can no longer switch to an alternative production version after release.

 

From our point of view, the level of detail also decreases if we want to be very flexible on the DM side and want to switch between similar resources/work centres in REO, as we then have to work with pool capacities on the S/4HANA side.

 

We would be happy to discuss how you deal with this issue.

 

Many thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - CDS View Entity Associations ( August Developer Challenge )
Flow Builder Plus: When to use Liquidity Item Online Derivation?
entry.content:
In recent releases for both Public Cloud and Private Cloud/OP release, there is one new functionality named 'Liquidity Item Online Derivation' has been added to Flow Builder.

For OP customer, that configuration can be reached via IMG path: 'SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Financial Supply Chain Management -> Cash and Liquidity Management -> Cash Management -> Data Setup -> Define Liquidity Item Derivation Mode for Flow Builder'.

The configuration provides a company code based configuration:

With this configuration, you can choose the liquidity item derivation mode with:

  • Derive Liquidity Item Online
  • Fetch Liquidity Item from table BSEG

By default, S/4 system treat all company codes as 'Fetch Liquidity Item from table BSEG'. And the liquidity item is derived for each accounting document line item during accounting document posting, and saved into table BSEG directly with field 'LQITEM'.

‘Derive Liquidity Item Online’ offers one additional approach, system won't read the Liquidity item information stored in accounting document line item but perform the liquidity item derivation when building the flows.

This new approach is in favor of certain ECC customers who prefer manual working to the automatic processing by system. In ECC era, Liquidity item will be recalculated every time when building flows, because liquidity item won’t store in accounting document line item.

Storing Liquidity item in accounting document line item gain lots of advantages, for example, it grant an automatic processing, and customer can check the correctness of liquidity item derivation logic by pass the liquidity item chain tracing logic. But it also have one certain disadvantage: you need perform one additional step to update liquidity item in accounting document line item via program FCLM_UPDATE_LQITEM. If you forget to run it, the liquidity item will stay unchanged in both accounting document line item table and flows.

But it does not mean the 'Online derivation logic' is perfect, it also got significant disadvantages:

  • Rebuild step is still a mandatory unless you expect your updated liquidity item derivation logic impacts only the new flows. Instead of updating liquidity item in accounting document line items, rebuild the existing flows to reflect the updated liquidity item derivation logic is a must. If you forget the rebuild the flows, the situation is the same.
  • It will hurt flows' building performance. If you have a complexity setup of query/query sequence, it may impact the performance much more.
  • It will make the trouble shooting more complicated, and you need firstly identify the issue located in liquidity item derivation or liquidity item chain tracing.

So, when to use Liquidity Item Online Derivation? 

I will suggest to enable 'Liquidity Item Online Derivation' for the following scenarios:

  • You are ECC customers, and you get used to the ECC data build approach. Means, you will build flows with a periodically manner.
  • You have a frequently updated liquidity item rule setup. Depends on the business needs, if the liquidity item per accounting document line item are unstable, saving liquidity item in BSEG making no sense because you have to rebuild them time to time.

 

For all other cases, I will suggest use the default liquidity item derivation and let the system perform the automatic operations, you will only manually change certain data via rebuild programs.

Finally, we understand that currently the two-steps rebuilding (rebuild liquidity item in accounting documents, then rebuild flows) is somehow the root reason for forgetting the rebuild. Maybe in a near future, we will reduce such rebuilding steps as 1, means, rebuilding flows automatically for those document items who got a new liquidity item.

Feel free to leave comment or contact me for better ideas. 😀
